q: What is the German constitution called?
ex: Germany's constitution is called the Grundgesetz.
q_de: Wie heißt die deutsche Verfassung?
ex_de: Die deutsche Verfassung heißt Grundgesetz.

q: Who elects the Bundestag?
ex: Citizens elect the Bundestag in democratic elections.
q_de: Wer wählt den Bundestag?
ex_de: Bürgerinnen und Bürger wählen den Bundestag in demokratischen Wahlen.

q: What is forbidden in a democracy?
ex: Election fraud damages free democratic elections.
q_de: Was ist in einer Demokratie verboten?
ex_de: Wahlbetrug schadet freien demokratischen Wahlen.

q: What do you do in an election?
choices: You ask the police to decide
ex: Voting is secret.
q_de: Was macht man bei einer Wahl?
choices_de: man bittet die Polizei zu entscheiden
ex_de: Wahlen sind geheim.

q: Why are several parties important?
choices: So different opinions can be represented
ex: Pluralism allows different political positions.
q_de: Warum sind mehrere Parteien wichtig?
ex_de: Pluralismus ermöglicht verschiedene politische Positionen.

q: What does freedom of the press mean?
choices: Newspapers need permission from one party
Media may report freely within the law
ex: Press freedom is a protected basic right.
q_de: Was bedeutet Pressefreiheit?
choices_de: Zeitungen brauchen die Erlaubnis einer Partei
Medien dürfen im Rahmen des Gesetzes frei berichten
ex_de: Pressefreiheit ist ein geschütztes Grundrecht.

q: Who can be a member of a political party?
choices: Citizens and residents according to party rules
ex: Political parties are open according to their statutes.
q_de: Wer kann Mitglied einer politischen Partei sein?
choices_de: Bürger und Einwohner nach den Parteiregeln
ex_de: Politische Parteien sind nach ihren Satzungen offen organisiert.

q: What does equality before the law mean?
choices: The same law applies regardless of origin or gender
Rich people may ignore the law
Men and women have different basic rights
ex: Everyone is equal before the law.
q_de: Was bedeutet Gleichheit vor dem Gesetz?
choices_de: Für alle gilt dasselbe Gesetz – unabhängig von Herkunft oder Geschlecht
reiche Menschen dürfen das Gesetz ignorieren
Männer und Frauen haben unterschiedliche Grundrechte
ex_de: Vor dem Gesetz sind alle gleich.

q: What is the role of the opposition in parliament?
choices: To control and criticise the government
ex: Opposition is part of democratic control.
q_de: Welche Aufgabe hat die Opposition im Parlament?
choices_de: die Regierung zu kontrollieren und zu kritisieren
ex_de: Die Opposition ist Teil der demokratischen Kontrolle.

q: What is guaranteed by freedom of religion?
choices: You may choose and practise a religion freely
ex: Freedom of religion protects individual choice.
q_de: Was garantiert die Religionsfreiheit?
choices_de: man darf eine Religion frei wählen und ausüben
ex_de: Die Religionsfreiheit schützt die freie persönliche Entscheidung.

q: What is a Land in Germany?
ex: Germany is a federal state made up of Länder.
q_de: Was ist ein Land in Deutschland?
ex_de: Deutschland ist ein Bundesstaat aus Ländern.

q: How many federal states does Germany have?
ex: Germany has 16 Länder.
q_de: Wie viele Bundesländer hat Deutschland?
ex_de: Deutschland hat 16 Bundesländer.

q: What is the capital of Germany?
ex: Berlin is the capital.
q_de: Was ist die Hauptstadt von Deutschland?
ex_de: Berlin ist die Hauptstadt.

q: What is the job of the police?
choices: To protect public safety and enforce the law
ex: Police protect safety and enforce the law.
q_de: Welche Aufgabe hat die Polizei?
choices_de: die öffentliche Sicherheit zu schützen und das Gesetz durchzusetzen
ex_de: Police protect safety and enforce the law.

q: What is the Federal Constitutional Court for?
choices: To check whether laws fit the constitution
ex: It protects the constitution.
q_de: Wofür ist das Bundesverfassungsgericht da?
choices_de: zu prüfen, ob Gesetze mit der Verfassung vereinbar sind
ex_de: It protects the constitution.

q: Who signs federal laws after they are passed?
ex: The Federal President signs laws.
q_de: Wer unterschreibt Bundesgesetze, nachdem sie beschlossen wurden?
ex_de: The Federal President signs laws.

q: What does federalism mean?
choices: All power is only in one city
Power is shared between federation and Länder
ex: Federalism divides responsibilities.
q_de: Was bedeutet Föderalismus?
choices_de: alle Macht liegt nur in einer Stadt
die Macht wird zwischen Bund und Ländern geteilt
ex_de: Federalism divides responsibilities.

q: What is a municipality responsible for?
choices: Local services like roads and registration
ex: Municipalities handle local administration.
q_de: Wofür ist eine Gemeinde zuständig?
choices_de: lokale Aufgaben wie Straßen und Anmeldung
ex_de: Municipalities handle local administration.

q: Where do you usually register your address?
ex: Address registration is done with local authorities.
q_de: Wo meldet man normalerweise seinen Wohnsitz an?
ex_de: Address registration is done with local authorities.

q: What is the Bundestag?
ex: The Bundestag is the federal parliament.
q_de: Was ist der Bundestag?
ex_de: The Bundestag is the federal parliament.

q: What is freedom of expression?
choices: You may express opinions within the law
You must repeat the opinion of your employer
ex: Freedom of expression protects opinion within legal limits.
q_de: Was ist Meinungsfreiheit?
choices_de: man darf im Rahmen des Gesetzes seine Meinung äußern
man muss die Meinung des Arbeitgebers wiederholen
ex_de: Freedom of expression protects opinion within legal limits.

q: What protects human dignity?
ex: Human dignity is protected by the Basic Law.
q_de: Was schützt die Menschenwürde?
ex_de: Human dignity is protected by the Basic Law.

q: What is not allowed even if someone is angry?
ex: Violence is illegal.
q_de: Was ist nicht erlaubt, auch wenn jemand wütend ist?
ex_de: Violence is illegal.

q: What does equal opportunity in education aim for?
choices: Access should not depend on origin
Schools should only exist in big cities
ex: Education should be accessible regardless of background.
q_de: Was will Chancengleichheit in der Bildung erreichen?
choices_de: Zugang soll nicht von der Herkunft abhängen
Schulen soll es nur in Großstädten geben
ex_de: Education should be accessible regardless of background.

q: What right helps workers organise together?
ex: Workers may organise in unions and associations.
q_de: Welches Recht hilft Arbeitnehmern, sich gemeinsam zu organisieren?
ex_de: Workers may organise in unions and associations.

q: What may you do if an office makes a wrong decision?
choices: Use legal remedies like an objection or lawsuit
ex: Administrative decisions can be challenged legally.
q_de: Was darf man tun, wenn ein Amt eine falsche Entscheidung trifft?
choices_de: Rechtsmittel wie Widerspruch oder Klage nutzen
ex_de: Administrative decisions can be challenged legally.

q: What is discrimination?
choices: Unfair unequal treatment because of traits like origin or religion
ex: Discrimination is unfair treatment.
q_de: Was ist Diskriminierung?
choices_de: ungerechte Benachteiligung wegen Merkmalen wie Herkunft oder Religion
ex_de: Discrimination is unfair treatment.

q: What is protected by the right to privacy?
ex: Privacy protects private life and data.
q_de: Was wird durch das Recht auf Privatsphäre geschützt?
ex_de: Privacy protects private life and data.

q: What may the state not do?
ex: Torture is prohibited.
q_de: Was darf der Staat nicht tun?
ex_de: Torture is prohibited.

q: What is the purpose of social rights and protections?
choices: To support people in need and protect social participation
ex: Social protections support participation and security.
q_de: Wozu dienen soziale Rechte und Schutzsysteme?
choices_de: Menschen in Not zu unterstützen und soziale Teilhabe zu schützen
ex_de: Social protections support participation and security.

q: What happened on 3 October?
ex: 3 October marks German reunification.
q_de: Was geschah am 3. Oktober?
ex_de: 3 October marks German reunification.

q: What happened in 1989 in Berlin?
ex: The fall of the Berlin Wall was a key event in 1989.
q_de: Was geschah 1989 in Berlin?
ex_de: The fall of the Berlin Wall was a key event in 1989.

q: What does “Nie wieder” refer to in German remembrance culture?
choices: A commitment against dictatorship and persecution
ex: It refers to responsibility after National Socialism.
q_de: Worauf bezieht sich „Nie wieder“ in der deutschen Erinnerungskultur?
choices_de: eine Verpflichtung gegen Diktatur und Verfolgung
ex_de: It refers to responsibility after National Socialism.

q: Why are memorials important?
choices: They remember victims and historical responsibility
ex: Memorials help preserve remembrance.
q_de: Warum sind Gedenkstätten wichtig?
choices_de: sie erinnern an die Opfer und an historische Verantwortung
ex_de: Memorials help preserve remembrance.

q: What was divided after the Second World War?
ex: Germany was divided into East and West.
q_de: Was war nach dem Zweiten Weltkrieg geteilt?
ex_de: Germany was divided into East and West.

q: What is a central lesson from the Nazi period?
choices: Human rights and democracy must be protected
ex: The Nazi period showed why democracy and rights matter.
q_de: Was ist eine zentrale Lehre aus der NS-Zeit?
choices_de: Menschenrechte und Demokratie müssen geschützt werden
ex_de: The Nazi period showed why democracy and rights matter.

q: What was the Berlin Wall?
choices: A border barrier dividing East and West Berlin
ex: The Wall divided Berlin until 1989.
q_de: Was war die Berliner Mauer?
choices_de: eine Grenzanlage zwischen Ost- und West-Berlin
ex_de: The Wall divided Berlin until 1989.

q: When did Germany become reunified?
ex: Reunification happened in 1990.
q_de: Wann wurde Deutschland wiedervereinigt?
ex_de: Reunification happened in 1990.

q: What does a democracy need from citizens?
ex: Democracy depends on active citizens.
q_de: Was braucht eine Demokratie von ihren Bürgern?
ex_de: Democracy depends on active citizens.

q: Why do schools teach National Socialist history?
choices: To understand responsibility and protect democracy
To replace history of other periods
ex: History teaching helps prevent repetition of injustice.
q_de: Warum wird in Schulen die Geschichte des Nationalsozialismus behandelt?
choices_de: um Verantwortung zu verstehen und Demokratie zu schützen
ex_de: History teaching helps prevent repetition of injustice.

q: What is compulsory schooling?
choices: Children must attend school for a number of years
ex: School attendance is compulsory.
q_de: Was bedeutet Schulpflicht?
choices_de: Kinder müssen mehrere Jahre zur Schule gehen
ex_de: School attendance is compulsory.

q: What is the purpose of health insurance?
ex: Health insurance covers medical treatment.
q_de: Wozu dient die Krankenversicherung?
ex_de: Health insurance covers medical treatment.

q: What should you do if you move house?
choices: Register your new address on time
ex: Address registration is required.
q_de: Was sollte man tun, wenn man umzieht?
ex_de: Address registration is required.

q: What can parents usually do in Germany?
ex: Parents may share care responsibilities.
q_de: Was können Eltern in Deutschland normalerweise tun?
ex_de: Parents may share care responsibilities.

q: What is a rental contract?
choices: An agreement between tenant and landlord
ex: A rental contract regulates renting a home.
q_de: Was ist ein Mietvertrag?
choices_de: eine Vereinbarung zwischen Mieter und Vermieter
ex_de: A rental contract regulates renting a home.

q: What is a trade union?
choices: A political party in every company
ex: Trade unions represent employees.
q_de: Was ist eine Gewerkschaft?
choices_de: eine politische Partei in jeder Firma
ex_de: Trade unions represent employees.

q: What may you do if you lose your job?
ex: The employment agency can support job seekers.
q_de: Was darf man tun, wenn man seine Arbeit verliert?
ex_de: The employment agency can support job seekers.

q: Why are taxes paid?
choices: To finance public tasks like schools and roads
ex: Taxes finance public services.
q_de: Warum zahlt man Steuern?
choices_de: um öffentliche Aufgaben wie Schulen und Straßen zu finanzieren
ex_de: Taxes finance public services.

q: What does integration mean?
choices: Participating in society with rights and responsibilities
Living without any contact to others
ex: Integration is participation in society.
q_de: Was bedeutet Integration?
choices_de: am gesellschaftlichen Leben mit Rechten und Pflichten teilzunehmen
die eigene Geschichte völlig zu vergessen
ohne jeden Kontakt zu anderen zu leben
ex_de: Integration is participation in society.

q: What should happen in a workplace team?
choices: Respect, cooperation and following the rules
ex: Workplaces require cooperation and respect.
q_de: Was sollte in einem Arbeitsteam passieren?
ex_de: Workplaces require cooperation and respect.

q: In Germany, what is the Basic Law?
ex: The Basic Law is the constitution of Germany.

ex_de: The Basic Law is the constitution of Germany.

q: Which statement about the Basic Law is correct?

q: Which option does equality before the law mean?

ex: Health insurance covers medical treatment.

choices: Participating in society with rights and responsibilities

choices: Respect, cooperation and following the rules
ex: Workplaces require cooperation and respect.

q: Which option best describes the Basic Law?
ex: The Basic Law is the constitution of Germany.

q: Which option best describes secret voting?

votes must be shown to neighbours

Stimmen müssen den Nachbarn gezeigt werden

q: Which option best describes freedom of assembly?
ex: Peaceful assembly is protected.

q: What does freedom of religion mean in Germany?
choices: Everyone may choose and practise a religion freely
Religion is decided by the employer
ex: Freedom of religion means people may choose, change and practise a religion freely within the law.
q_de: Was bedeutet freedom of religion in Deutschland?
choices_de: Everyone may choose and practise a religion freely
Religion is decided by the employer
ex_de: Freedom of religion means people may choose, change and practise a religion freely within the law.

q: How often are Bundestag elections normally held?
ex: The Bundestag is normally elected every four years.

q: Which colors are on the German flag?
ex: The German flag is black, red and gold.
q_de: Welche Farben hat die deutsche Flagge?
ex_de: The German flag is black, red and gold.

q: What is the role of the Bundesrat?
ex: The Bundesrat represents the Länder in federal lawmaking.
q_de: Welche Aufgabe hat der Bundesrat?
ex_de: The Bundesrat represents the Länder in federal lawmaking.

q: What does the rule of law mean?
choices: The government may ignore the courts
State action is bound by law
Only police decide what is legal
ex: In a Rechtsstaat, state institutions are bound by law and can be checked by courts.
q_de: Was bedeutet Rechtsstaatlichkeit?
choices_de: The government may ignore the courts
State action is bound by law
Only police decide what is legal
ex_de: In a Rechtsstaat, state institutions are bound by law and can be checked by courts.

q: Who can join a political party in Germany?
choices: Adults who meet the legal requirements
ex: Political participation through parties is generally open to adults who meet the legal requirements.
q_de: Wer kann in Deutschland einer politischen Partei beitreten?
choices_de: Erwachsene, die die gesetzlichen Voraussetzungen erfüllen
ex_de: Political participation through parties is generally open to adults who meet the legal requirements.

q: What is protected by freedom of opinion?
ex: Freedom of opinion protects expressing views within the limits of the law.
q_de: Was schützt die Meinungsfreiheit?
ex_de: Freedom of opinion protects expressing views within the limits of the law.

q: Why are taxes important for the state?
choices: They are paid only by tourists
ex: Taxes help finance schools, roads, administration and many public services.
q_de: Warum sind Steuern für den Staat wichtig?
choices_de: They are paid only by tourists
ex_de: Taxes help finance schools, roads, administration and many public services.

q: Which institution decides whether a law is constitutional?
ex: The Bundesverfassungsgericht reviews constitutional questions.
q_de: Welche Institution entscheidet, ob ein Gesetz verfassungsgemäß ist?
ex_de: The Bundesverfassungsgericht reviews constitutional questions.

q: What is equal treatment before the law?
choices: Everyone is judged by the same laws
ex: Equality before the law means the same legal standards apply to everyone.
q_de: Was bedeutet Gleichbehandlung vor dem Gesetz?
ex_de: Equality before the law means the same legal standards apply to everyone.

q: Which level of government is responsible for local matters like a town hall?
ex: Local matters are mainly handled by municipalities.
q_de: Welche staatliche Ebene ist für lokale Angelegenheiten wie das Rathaus zuständig?

q: What is one important task of the police in a democracy?
choices: Protect public safety under the law
ex: The police protect public safety and must act within the law.
q_de: Was ist eine wichtige Aufgabe der Polizei in einer Demokratie?
choices_de: Protect public safety under the law
ex_de: The police protect public safety and must act within the law.

q: What may citizens do in a democracy?
choices: Take part in elections and public discussion
Choose judges directly in every case
ex: Citizens participate through voting and public debate.
q_de: Was dürfen Bürger in einer Demokratie tun?
choices_de: an Wahlen und öffentlichen Diskussionen teilnehmen
in jedem Fall Richter direkt wählen
ex_de: Citizens participate through voting and public debate.

q: Why is an independent press important?
choices: It informs the public and can criticise power
ex: A free press helps control political power by informing the public.
q_de: Warum ist eine unabhängige Presse wichtig?
choices_de: sie informiert die Öffentlichkeit und kann Macht kritisieren
ex_de: A free press helps control political power by informing the public.

q: What does social security support?
choices: People in situations like illness, unemployment or old age
ex: The social system supports people in specific life situations.
q_de: Wobei unterstützt die soziale Sicherung?
choices_de: Menschen in Situationen wie Krankheit, Arbeitslosigkeit oder Alter
ex_de: The social system supports people in specific life situations.

q: What happens if someone does not agree with an administrative decision?
choices: They can often use legal remedies
They must accept it immediately in every case
ex: Legal remedies and courts are part of the rule of law.
q_de: Was passiert, wenn jemand mit einer Behördenentscheidung nicht einverstanden ist?
choices_de: sie müssen es in jedem Fall sofort akzeptieren
ex_de: Legal remedies and courts are part of the rule of law.

q: What is the purpose of the Constitution?
choices: It defines fundamental rights and the state order
ex: A constitution structures the state and protects fundamental rights.
q_de: Wozu dient eine Verfassung?
choices_de: sie legt Grundrechte und die Staatsordnung fest
ex_de: A constitution structures the state and protects fundamental rights.

q: What does gender equality mean?
choices: Women and men have equal rights
ex: Equal rights for women and men are protected in Germany.
q_de: Was bedeutet Gleichberechtigung von Frauen und Männern?
choices_de: Frauen und Männer haben gleiche Rechte
ex_de: Equal rights for women and men are protected in Germany.

q: What is volunteering?
ex: Volunteering means people freely contribute time and effort.
q_de: Was ist Ehrenamt?
ex_de: Volunteering means people freely contribute time and effort.

q: What can help integration?
ex: Language, contact and participation support integration.
q_de: Was kann Integration fördern?
ex_de: Language, contact and participation support integration.
